channel.title: Breads on Bell Favorite Recipes
channel.description: Recent content in Breads on Bell Favorite Recipes

item.title: Wild Rice Many-Grain Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup wild rice
  • 2 Tbsp granulated sugar
  • ½ cup warm water
  • 1 pkg dry yeast (or 1 Tbsp )
  • 5 cups (approx.) all-purpose flour
  • ¼ cup fancy molasses
  • 2 Tbsp shortening
  • 1 tsp salt
  • ½ cup natural bran
  • ½ cup raw sunflower seeds
  • ¾ cup 7-grain cereal
  • ¾ cup rolled oats

Instructions

  1.  In saucepan, cook wild rice in 2 cups water (500 ml) water for about 45 minutes or until tender; drain and let cool.

  2.  Meanwhile, dissolve 1 tsp (5 ml) of the sugar in warm water. Sprinkle in yeast; let soften, stirring together until dissolved. Stir in ¼ cup (50 ml) of the flour to make a thin paste; set in warm place for 10 minutes or until bubbly.

  3.  In large bowl and using electric mixer, combine molasses, remaining sugar, shortening, salt and 2 cups (500 ml) warm water. In order, beat in bran, sunflower seeds, yeast mixture, wild rice, 7-grain cereal and rolled oats. With wooden spoon, beat in as much of the remaining flour as necessary to make firm dough. Turn out onto lightly floured surface; knead for about 10 minutes or until smooth and elastic, kneading in as much of the remaining flour as necessary..

  4.  Place in large greased bowl, turning to grease all over. Cover with plastic wrap; let rice in warm draft-free place until doubled in bulk, about 2 hours. (NOTE: I tried the plastic wrap and found the dough stuck to it even if it was lightly greased; I choose instead to make sure that there is flour on the surface and then cover it with a clean cloth.)

  5.  Punch down dough; turn out onto lightly floured surface. Divide into thirds. With rolling pin, flatten each to about 7-1/2-inch (19 cm) square; firmly roll up each into log. Place each in greased 8- x  4-inch pans. Cover and let rise until doubled in bulk, about 1-1/2 hours.

  6.  Bake in center of 375 F (190 C) oven for 15 minutes or until loaves sound hollow when tapped on bottom. Remove from pans; let cool on racks. Makes 3 loaves, 12 slices each.

item.title: Water Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup lukewarm water
  • 1 tsp sugar
  • 3 cups (2 boiling hot & 1 cold) water
  • 2 tbsp sugar
  • 1 tbsp salt
  • 1 tbsp dry yeast or 1 envelope
  • 7 – 8 cups all-purpose flour     (6 measured out to start with, then technique determines the rest - see below)
  • ¼ cup shortening or margarine

Instructions

Pour 2 cups boiling water on salt, 2 tbsp sugar and shortening in bowl.  When shortening has melted, add 1 cup cold water.  Prepare yeast (see NOTE below).

item.title: Toasty Garlic Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 French bread, 12 to 15”, cut into 1”cubes
  • ½ cup butter or margarine, softened
  • Parsley flakes
  • Grated Parmesan cheese
  • Garlic powder

Instructions

Spread both sides of bread with butter. Place, cut side down, on a cookie sheet. Sprinkle one side with cheese, parsley and garlic powder. Broil 4 inches from the heat until light golden brown. Turn slices and repeat with other side. Serve immediately.

item.title: Tea Biscuits Supreme
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups stirred all-purpose flour
  • 2 tsp sugar
  • 4 tsp baking powder
  • ½ cup butter of shortening
  • ½ tsp cream of tartar
  • 2/3 cup milk
  • ½ tsp salt

Instructions

Sift together into mixing bowl the flour, baking powder, cream of tartar, salt and sugar. Cut in butter (or fat of your choice) with pastry blender until size of peas. Add milk all at once and quickly stir in.

Turn out onto flour sprinkled board. Pat smoothly to ¾ inch thickness and cut with 2-inch floured cutter. Bake on ungreased baking sheet at 450 degrees F. for 10 minutes.

item.title: Tea Biscuit Mix
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 8 cups sifted all-purpose flour
  • 5 Tbsp baking powder
  • 2 tsp salt
  • 1 cup skim milk powder
  • 1-1-2 cup shortening

Instructions

TEA BISCUIT MIX: Combine first 4 ingredients. Mix in shortening until it resembles fine bread crumbs. May be stored in covered container in refrigerator or cool place. Makes about 12 cups.

TEA BISCUITS: Combine Tea Biscuit Mix and water. Knead gently on floured board. Roll 1/3 inch thick; cut into triangles. Bake on ungreased cookie sheet for 10 minutes at 450 F.

item.title: Stuffing Balls
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1/3 cup butter or margarine
  • ½ cup chopped celery
  • ¼ cup chopped onion
  • 5 cups soft bread cubes
  • 1 cup chopped walnuts
  • 1 tsp salt
  • ½ tsp poultry seasoning
  • ¼ tsp pepper
  • ½ cup chopped parsley
  • ¼ cup chicken broth
  • 1 egg, well beaten

Instructions

In a skillet, melt butter. Saute celery and onion until tender. Combine remaining ingredients and shape mixture into eight balls. Place on a greased baking sheet. Bake at 375 degrees for about 20 minutes.

item.title: Red Lobster Cheddar Bay Biscuits
item.description:
• 2-1/2 cups Bisquick baking mix
  • 4 Tbs cold butter or margarine
  • 1 heaping cup grated cheddar cheese
  • ¾ cup cold whole milk
  • ¼ tsp garlic powder

TOPPING:

Ingredients

  • 2 Tbs butter
  • ½ tsp garlic powder
  • ¼ tsp dried parsley flakes
  • Pinch of salt

Yield: 1 dozen biscuits

Combine Bisquick with cold butter in medium bowl, mixing with pastry cutter or fork until remaining chunks of butter are size of peas. Add cheddar cheese, milk and ¼ tsp garlic powder. Mix by hand until combined but don’t overmix.

item.title: Purity Tea Biscuits
item.description:
A Tea Biscuit dough should be soft but not sticky. Little kneading improves the quality of the biscuits, but should be done with a gentle touch as too much handling tends to make them tough.

Preheat oven to 450 F. Blend or sift together:

Ingredients

  • 2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 4 tsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp salt

Instructions

Cut finely 1/3 cup shortening (or ¼ cup oil).

Add ¾ cup plus 2 Tbsp milk (200 ml).

item.title: Raisin Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup lukewarm water
  • 1 pkg or 1 Tbsp yeast
  • 1 tsp sugar
  • 3 cups tepid water (2 boiling, 1 cold)
  • ¼ cup shortening
  • ½ cup sugar
  • 2 tsp cinnamon
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 2 beaten eggs
  • 1 cup lightly floured raisins
  • Flour

Instructions

Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Dissolve sugar and yeast in 1stquantity of water

Melt ½ cup sugar, shortening and salt in 2 cups boiling water, then cool mixture with the one cup of cold water.

item.title: Purity Pancakes
item.description:
Blend together:

  • 1-1/2 cups flour
  • 1 – 2 tbsp sugar
  • 3 tsp baking powder
  • 1/8 tsp salt

Combine:

Ingredients

  • 1 beaten egg
  • 1-3/4 cup milk
  • 2 tbsp vegetable oil or melted shortening

Instructions

Add liquids to the dry ingredients. Beat only until combined. Bake in a hot skillet slightly greased until edge of pancake begins to dry and lose its gloss. Turn and bake until golden brown.

NOTE: If batter thickens, add a little more milk.

item.title: Pizza Pie Dough
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cup Bisquick
  • 1/3 cup very hot water

Instructions

Heat oven to 450 degrees. Grease cookie sheet or 12-inch pizza pan. Mix 1-1/2 cups Bisquick baking mix and 1/3 cup very hot water; beat vigorously 20 strokes. Turn dough onto surface well dusted with baking mix. Knead about 60 times or until smooth and no longer sticky. With hands dusted with baking mix, press dough into 13-inch circle on cookie sheet or press in pizza pan; pinch edge, forming ½ inch rim. Top pizza to taste. Bake 12 to 15 minutes or until crust is brown and cheese is melted and bubbly.

item.title: Pizza Buns
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup vegetable oil
  • 1 minced onion
  • 1 cup spaghetti sauce or 8-oz tomato sauce
  • 1 lb grated cheddar cheese
  • 1 tin mushroom pieces, drained
  • 1 tsp oregano
  • ½ tsp salt
  • Dash pepper
  • Dash garlic powder

Instructions

MIX. Spread on buns and broil till bubbly.

Sauce keeps for a month.

item.title: Pearl's Zucchini Loaf
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 eggs
  • 1 cup oil
  • 2 cups white sugar
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 2 cups shredded raw zucchini
  • 3 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp each salt, baking powder
  • 1 tsp cinnamon or 2 tsp nutmeg
  • 1/2 tsp baking soda
  • 1/2 cup chopped nuts
  • Raisins, if desired

Instructions

Line two 4”x 8”loaf pans (or one large pan) with greased waxed paper. Combine first 5 ingredients; add mixed dry ingredients and stir well. Pour into loaf pans and bake at 325 degrees for approx. l hour. Test to make sure loaf is baked right through.

item.title: Parker House Buns
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 cups lukewarm water
  • 4 tsp sugar
  • 3 pkg or 3 Tbsp yeast
  • 3 eggs
  • ½ cup melted shortening
  • ½ cup plus 3 tsp sugar
  • 9 cups (more or less) flour

Instructions

Add water and sugar together then sprinkle yeast over top. Let rise.

Mix eggs, shortening and 2ndamount of sugar together. Add yeast mixture and flour.

Knead. Let rise l to 1-1/2 hours. Place two finger-sized pieces of dough in muffin tin.

item.title: Minute Magic Tea Biscuits
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups all-purpose flour (sifted)
  • 1/3 cup St Lawrence Oil
  • 3 tsp baking powder
  • 2/3 cup milk
  • 1 tsp salt

Instructions

Sift together flour, salt & baking powder. Add oil and milk. Stir with a fork until all the dry ingredients are moistened. Place the dough on a piece of wax paper. Dampen the working surface so that the paper won’t slip. Cover the dough with wax paper and roll it with a rolling pin to ½” thickness. Peel off wax paper. Cut into biscuits. Place on an ungreased baking sheet. Bake in a very hot oven 475 degrees F. for 10 to 12 minutes. Yields about 15 med biscuits.

item.title: Old-Fashioned Bread Dressing
item.description:
Old-Fashioned Bread Dressing (Stuffing)

Ingredients

  • ½ cup chopped onion
  • ½ cup butter or margarine
  • 1 tsp poultry seasoning
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1/8 tsp pepper
  • 8 cups dry bread cubes1 cup chicken broth or water

NOTE: (I (Mom) use 1 tsp chicken bouillon powder in 1 cup boiling water.)

Saute onions in margarine. Add all other ingredients and mix well. This amount stuffs a 10-lb turkey or can be heated in a greased foil-covered bread pan in a 325 degree oven for 40-45 minutes if it has been refrigerated, or at a lower temperature for a longer period.

item.title: Magic Sweet Yeast Bread
item.description:
Combine in a cup:

  • 1/2 cup lukewarm Water
  • 2 tsp sugar

Sprinkle over the top:

  • 2 pkg dry yeast

Let soften for about 15 minutes.

Scald in a saucepan over boiling water:

  • 2/3 cup Milk

Mix in & Set aside to cool:

  • ½ cup St. Lawrence Oil
  • ½ cup Bee Hive Corn Syrup

Measure and sift together:

Ingredients

  • 5-1/2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp salt
  • ¼ cup sugar

Instructions

Combine with the milk, corn oil and syrup mixture …5 beaten eggs and 2 cups of the sifted flour, sugar, salt mixture. Mix to give a thick batter. Beat vigorously and stir in yeast.

item.title: Magic Chelsea Buns
item.description:
Roll out one portion of the Sweet Yeast Bread (recipe foundHERE) to a large rectangle 14” x 10”. Spread with the following mixture to within about ½” of the edge.

Ingredients

  • ¼ cup Bee Hive Corn Syrup
  • 2 tbsp St. Lawrence Oil
  • ¼ cup brown sugar
  • 1 tsp cinnamon

Instructions

Roll up tightly starting at the long side of the rectangle. Into each of 12 muffins pour 1 tsp St. Lawrence Oil and 1 tsp

item.title: Johnny Cake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup flour
  • 2 Tbsp sugar
  • 4 tsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1 cup cornmeal
  • 1 egg
  • 1 cup milk
  • 1 Tbsp melted butter

Instructions

Mix and sift flour, sugar, baking powder and salt. Add cornmeal. Beat egg and milk and add to the flour mixture. Mix thoroughly and add melted butter.

Bake in a greased cake pan 8-inch square at 350 F. for 30 minutes.

item.title: Jam Buns
item.description:
• 2 cups flour
  • 1 cup shortening
  • 3 tsp baking powder
  • 2 tbsp sugar

MIX LIKE PASTRY AND THEN ADD;

Ingredients

  • 1 egg
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • ½ cup milk

Instructions

Mix well. Roll out on floured board. Divide evenly and place in muffin tins. Put about 1 tbsp jam in center of each and fold corners together. Bake at 350 degrees for 15 minutes.

item.title: Drop Doughnuts (Revolving Spudniks)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 eggs
  • ¾ cup white sugar
  • 1 cup milk
  • 3 cups cake or pastry flour OR 2-5/8 cups All-Purpose flour
  • 1 Tbsp baking powder
  • 1-1/2 tsp salt (I use ½ tsp)
  • ½ tsp nutmeg
  • 1 Tbsp melted margarine or butter

Instructions

BEAT eggs and sugar in beater bowl until pale – about 5 minutes. Sift together flour, baking powder, salt and nutmeg. Add them alternately with the milk to the bowl and beat until blended. Beat in melted butter or margarine.

item.title: Darryl's Lemon Loaf
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup shortening
  • 1 cup sugar
  • 2 eggs
  • 1 grated rind of lemon or 2-3 tsp flavor
  • 1-1/2 cup flour
  • ½ cup milk
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1 tsp baking powder

Instructions

Cream sugar and shortening. Add eggs and beat well. Add rind. Mix in dry ingredients with milk. Pour into greased bread pan. Bake at 350 degrees for 50 – 60 minutes until golden brown and toothpick comes out clean. While loaf is still hot, poke holes and spread glaze on loaf.

item.title: Doughnuts
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup sugar
  • 3 eggs
  • ½ cup less 1 tbsp soft butter
  • 6 tsp (2 Tbsp) baking powder
  • 4 cups sifted all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp salt
  • ½ tsp nutmeg
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 1 cup milk

Instructions

Into beater bowl measure the sugar, eggs and butter and beat until blended and smooth. Stir and measure the flour, add the baking powder, salt and nutmeg to it and sift again Add to first mixture along with the vanilla and milk and beat until smooth. Scrape down sides of bowl. Cover snugly and refrigerate four hours or overnight. Cut dough into quarters, scoop out ¼ with rubber scraper and out on generously floured board to ¼” thickness. (Keep remainder of dough in refrigerator.

item.title: Cranberry Christmas Gingerbread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cup sifted all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp ground ginger
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • ½ cup butter, softened
  • ½ cup sugar
  • 1/3 cup whole-berry cranberry sauce
  • 1 egg
  • ½ cup unsulfured molasses
  • ½ cup boiling water

Instructions

Grease and flour an 8” x 8” baking pan. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Sift together flour, baking soda, ginger and salt. Set aside. Using an electric mixer in a large bowl, cream butter and sugar until light and fluffy.

item.title: Corn Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup flour, sifted
  • ½ cup yellow corn meal
  • 3 tsp baking powder
  • 1 cup milk
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1 egg, beaten
  • ½ cup sugar
  • 1 Tbsp melted shortening

Instructions

Mix and sift flour, baking powder, salt and sugar. Stir in corn meal. Add milk to beaten egg and stir into first mixture. Add shortening and blend. Turn into shallow greased 8-inch pan. Bake at 400 degrees for 20 minutes.

item.title: Cheese Bread
item.description:
Credit for this recipe goes to Table Treasures.

Ingredients

  • ½ cup chopped onion
  • 1 Tbsp butter
  • 1 egg
  • ½ cup milk
  • 1-1/2 cup Bisquick
  • ½ cup grated cheese

Instructions

Saute onion in butter. Mix egg in milk. Add Bisquick and grated cheese, then onion. Stir only until mixed well. Pour into buttered 1-1/2 quart pan. Top with more cheese or poppy seed if desired. Bake at 400 F. for 20 – 25 minutes.

item.title: Butterhorns
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 Pkg or 1 Tbsp Yeast
  • ¼ cup lukewarm water
  • 2 eggs, plus enough scalded & cooled milk (or cooled boiled water) to make 2 cups (beat this slightly)
  • ½ cup (1/4 lb)melted butter or margarine
  • 1 tsp salt
  • ½ cup sugar
  • 6 cups all-purpose flour

Instructions

In large bowl sprinkle yeast & add warm water & stir until dissolved. Add eggs, milk (or water), butter, salt, sugar and stir until well blended.

item.title: Bran Muffins
item.description:
Credit for this recipe goes to Clara Therrien

Ingredients

  • 1 cup All-Bran
  • ½ cup sugar
  • ½ cup raisins
  • 2 tsp baking powder
  • 1 cup milk
  • 1 cup flour
  • ½ cup butter
  • Pinch salt
  • 1 egg

Instructions

Mix all together and put into muffin tin. Bake at 400 F. for 15 to 20 minutes.

item.title: Bacon Corn and Cheddar Bay Biscuits
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup flour
  • 7/8 cup milk
  • 4 tsp baking powder
  • 2 Tbsp milted shortening OR melted bacon fat
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 4 Tbsp brown sugar
  • ½ cup corn kernels
  • 1 cup corn meal
  • 6 bacon slices
  • 1 egg

Instructions

Preheat oven to 400 degrees. Line an 8x8x2-inch pan with bacon slices.

Sift flour, baking powder, salt and brown sugar. Add corn meal and stir to blend.

Beat egg, add milk, melted fat and corn kernels and stir to blend.

item.title: Banana Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 bananas, mashed
  • 1 cup white sugar
  • 1 egg, beaten
  • 1-1/2 cup flour
  • 1 tsp each baking soda, baking powder
  • 1/8 tsp salt
  • 2 tsp melted butter or oil
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Instructions

Mash bananas and mix with white sugar. Add beaten egg. Sift together flour, baking soda, baking powder and salt. Add to first mixture. Beat well. Add melted butter and vanilla. Bake in a greased loaf pan at 350 degrees for 35 to 45 minutes. Test to make sure it is baked right through.
